url.description: Absolute http/https URL to call. Private and localhost targets are blocked.

headers.description: Additional request headers. Transport headers like Host are blocked.

cookies.description: Cookie key/value pairs serialized into the Cookie header.

timeout_ms.description: Per-request timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to 30000.

follow_redirects.description: Whether to follow redirects. Defaults to true.

actions.title: request
actions.excerpt: Make a generic outbound HTTP request to a public API over `http` or `https`. Use this when you need a direct API call and there is no provider-specific Deepline integration yet. Input notes: - `url` must be an absolute p...
actions.content:
Make a generic outbound HTTP request to a public API over `http` or `https`.

Use this when you need a direct API call and there is no provider-specific Deepline integration yet.

Input notes:
- `url` must be an absolute public URL. Localhost, link-local, and private-network targets are blocked.
- `method` defaults to `GET`.
- `query` merges with any query params already present in `url`.
- `headers` accepts custom headers, except transport headers like `Host`, `Content-Length`, `Transfer-Encoding`, and `Connection`.
- `cookies` serializes into the `Cookie` header.
- Use exactly one body field:
  - `body_json`
  - `body_text`
  - `body_form_urlencoded`
- `GET` and `HEAD` requests cannot include a body.
- `follow_redirects` defaults to `true`.
- `timeout_ms` defaults to `30000` and is capped at `120000`.

Safety and platform limits:
- Deepline enforces a shared `5 requests/second` rate limit on this provider.
- This tool is intended for public internet APIs, not internal network access.

Response shape:
- `status_code`
- `ok`
- `headers`
- `data` (parsed JSON when possible, otherwise text)

When used in `deepline enrich`:
- The stored column value is Deepline's standard result envelope, not just the raw upstream body.
- Access the upstream response body from JavaScript as `row.<alias>.result.data`.
- Access Deepline miss metadata as `row.<alias>.__dl`.

Example `run_javascript` access pattern:

```js
return {
  ok: row.http_result?.result?.ok ?? null,
  status: row.http_result?.result?.status_code ?? null,
  upstream: row.http_result?.result?.data ?? null,
  miss: row.http_result?.__dl?.miss ?? null,
};
```
